data.title: Genesis renews as title partner of the Genesis Invitational with a multi-year agreement ahead of event's 100th playing.
data.description: Renowned golfer Tiger Woods has revealed a multi-year partnership agreement leading up to the 100th edition of a historic tournament. The deal was disclosed in Pacific Palisades, Calif., and Ponte Vedra Beach, Fla., on Feb. 17, 2026. Genesis, The PGA TOUR, and TGR Live shared news of Genesis extending its commitment for several years.

data.title: Crypto update: Stay informed with latest news on Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3, and crypto regulation.
data.description: Looking for the latest updates in the crypto world? Get the scoop on today's trends and events that are shaping the prices of Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. Stay informed about developments in blockchain technology, decentralized finance (DeFi), non-fungible tokens (NFTs), Web3, and regulations affecting the crypto market.

data.title: PetBux launches cashback marketplace for pet parents as pet ownership costs soar, offering savings on essentials.
data.description: A cashback marketplace designed for pet owners, known as PetBux, has recently been introduced in the United States, headquartered in Irvine, California. Launched on February 17, 2026, this platform aims to assist families in saving money on pet food, treats, and other necessary items. PetBux offers real cashback opportunities nationwide, catering exclusively to pet parents in the US.

data.title: Stock futures decline after Nikkei 225 breaks 57,000 points; STMicroelectronics up 5.93%, while Strategy Inc. falls 3.65%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures declined on Monday after a rebound on Friday, with major benchmark indices lower. The Nikkei 225 surpassed 57,000 points for the first time due to Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's election win and an endorsement from President Donald Trump. Important earnings reports scheduled include Coca-Cola, McDonald's, Ford, and T-Mobile. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.22%, and the two-year bond was at 3.50%. Markets are pricing an 84.2% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates in March. Index Performance: Dow Jones -0.12%, S&P 500 -0.37%, Nasdaq 100 -0.63%, Russell 2000 -0.33%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was up 0.002% at $690.77, while the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF declined 0.038% to $609.42. Strategy Inc. dropped 3.65% premarket due to a $12.4 billion net loss from Bitcoin declines. STMicroelectronics surged 5.93% after securing a multibillion-dollar deal with Amazon.

data.title: BlackRock's Ethereum ETF will take 18% of staking rewards, with Coinbase as prime execution agent. BlackRock also charges 0.25% annual sponsor fee.
data.description: BlackRock (NYSE: BLK) has updated its S-1 filing for the iShares Staked Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) Trust ETF, detailing an 18% staking reward split between the sponsor and prime execution agent. The amended filing highlights a 18% Staking Fee structure, with the trust keeping the remaining stake after the fee deduction. This 18% comprises BlackRock's staking-related portion and the prime execution agent's cut. The prime execution agent has the authority to distribute parts of the fee to staking service providers. Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN) functions as the custodian via Coinbase Custody Trust Company and the prime execution agent, playing a significant role in the staking process. Additionally, BlackRock imposes a 0.25% annual sponsor fee.

data.title: Wall Street rebounds on lower inflation rate, with small caps leading with Russell 2000 up 1.8%. Gold up 2%, silver up 4%. BTC rallies 4.6%.
data.description: Following a significant tech selloff on Thursday, Wall Street experienced a rebound driven by a weaker-than-anticipated inflation report, reinforcing expectations of potential interest rate cuts throughout the year. Annual inflation decreased from 2.7% to 2.4% in January, falling short of the projected 2.5% and hitting its lowest level since May 2025. Core inflation, excluding food and energy prices, also saw a decrease from 2.7% to 2.5% year over year, the lowest rate since March 2021. The Russell 2000, a small-cap stock benchmark, led the market recovery with a nearly 1.8% increase, surpassing its larger counterparts. In commodities trading, gold surged by 2% to trade above $5,000 in an effort to close the week on a positive note. Silver prices saw a substantial 4% surge to exceed $77 per ounce. Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) experienced a 4.6% rally, potentially ending a four-day decline streak. Positive corporate earnings further supported the market, with Applied Materials Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT) soaring by 9% after announcing strong quarterly results. Arista Networks Inc. (NYSE) also witnessed significant growth.

data.title: Blockchain venture capital shifting focus from narrative-driven token bets to real revenue infrastructure. $8 billion invested in crypto in 2022, most since 2017. Bitcoin losses may impact future investments. Traditional finance embracing tokenization of real assets.
data.description: The article discusses the shifting focus of venture capital in the crypto space, highlighting a move towards investments in projects with real revenue potential rather than narrative-driven token bets. The rise of stablecoins and real-world asset tokenization is emphasized, with notable figures like Gil Rosen claiming that the narrative-driven token projects are no longer sustainable. Despite this shift, venture capital firms have still invested around $8 billion in crypto and blockchain-related companies, the highest amount since 2022. However, deal counts remain below previous years, and Bitcoin's recent losses may impact future investments in the sector. The article also mentions a significant liquidation cascade in October, resulting in over $20 billion in notional positions being wiped out. The traditional finance sector is increasingly involved in blockchain technology, with a growing adoption of tokenization of real-world assets. Larry Fink of BlackRock emphasized the necessity of tokenization at the World Economic Forum in January.

data.title: Iridium (Nasdaq: IRDM) to attend Barclays Communications and Content Symposium on Feb. 17, 2026. Leading global satellite provider.
data.description: In McLean, Virginia, on February 17, 2026, Iridium Communications Inc. (Nasdaq: IRDM), a prominent provider of global satellite services for voice, data, and PNT, disclosed their involvement in upcoming investor events. They will be participating in the Barclays Communications and Content Symposium. This announcement marks a significant step for Iridium as they continue to strengthen their presence in the industry.

data.title: Land survey expert Scott Andersen explains role of land surveys in property decisions in HelloNation article.
data.description: In Menasha, Wisconsin on February 17, 2026, HelloNation released an article discussing the significance of a land survey in property decision-making. The piece includes input from Land Survey Expert Scott Andersen from Davel Engineering. This information highlights the importance of land surveys in making informed property decisions.

data.title: Dr. Jade Wu discusses the impact of sleep on stress, reaction time, creativity, and relationships. Sleep shapes various aspects of our lives.
data.description: A recent report reveals that sleep plays a crucial role beyond just determining fatigue levels. This study, conducted in Los Angeles on February 17, 2026, emphasizes the significant impact of sleep on stress interpretation, reaction time, creative thinking, and social connections. The findings highlight the intricate relationship between sleep quality and various cognitive and emotional functions. This research underscores the importance of adequate sleep in maintaining optimal mental and emotional well-being.

data.title: BirchStreet introduces AI-powered Smart AP to streamline P2P and accounting with line-item matching.
data.description: BirchStreet Systems, a leading global provider of hospitality operations technology, has introduced BirchStreet Smart AP™ in Las Vegas on Feb. 17, 2026. This innovative solution allows for the matching of purchase orders, receiving, and invoices at the line-item level. This marks a significant advancement in streamlining processes for businesses.

data.title: CAMS chosen to provide services for Nexus Renewable Power's Goody Solar + Storage Project in Texas.
data.description: A company based in Houston, CAMS, specializing in asset management, has been chosen by Nexus Renewable Power to offer various services for the "Goody" solar and storage venture in Lamar County, Texas.

data.title: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust announces February cash distribution; includes Cash Reserve paragraph.
data.description: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust recently announced their cash distribution for February. This update, released on 17-Feb-2026, included a new paragraph regarding their Cash Reserve. The trust declared a cash payment of $0.15 per unit to shareholders of record on February 27, 2026. This distribution reflects a decrease from the previous month's payment of $0.18 per unit. Shareholders can expect to receive their payments by March 12, 2026. The Trust continues to maintain a Cash Reserve fund which currently stands at $1.3 million. This fund is designed to provide financial stability and support future distributions to shareholders.

data.title: Energy stocks are rallying despite stable oil prices, driven by strong earnings (Exxon Mobil, Chevron) and investor rotation into cash-rich sectors. Resilience in margins and dividends make energy a preferred choice.
data.description: Energy stocks have been performing well recently despite stable crude oil prices. Companies like Exxon Mobil Corp. (XOM) and Chevron Corp. (CVX) have seen their shares rise due to strong quarterly earnings and margin resilience. Cost-cutting efforts and diversified revenue streams have helped these firms maintain high margins. Additionally, investor sentiment has shifted towards defensive, cash-rich sectors like energy, leading to a rotation of capital into these stocks. The high dividend yields and share buyback programs offered by energy companies are attractive to investors seeking income and balance in their portfolios. This shift in investor preferences has driven demand for energy shares, independent of oil price movements.

data.title: Air Duct Cleaning Expert Wayne Johnson advises Minnesota homeowners on scheduling air duct cleaning for smooth heating systems in long winters.
data.description: In Little Canada, Minnesota, on February 17, 2026, a HelloNation article advises homeowners on the frequency of air duct cleaning to ensure optimal performance of their heating systems during harsh winters. According to the article, experts from Air Duct Cleaning recommend scheduling regular cleanings to maintain efficiency. This guidance aims to help residents navigate the challenges of winter by facilitating the smooth operation of their heating systems.

data.title: Chewy CEO to speak at Morgan Stanley Conference on March 2, 2026 at 4:50 PM PT. Live webcast available. #CHWY #investorrelations
data.description: Chewy, Inc. revealed that Sumit Singh, CEO, is scheduled to engage in a fireside discussion at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 2, 2026, at 4:50 PM PT. The discussion will be available to stream live on the company's investor relations site at https://investor.chewy.com. Additionally, a playback of the session will be available for 90 days post-event. Chewy is known as a top destination for pet parents and partners, listed on the NYSE under the symbol CHWY.

data.title: Sandler Partners launches SCOUT email for faster customer responses on connectivity. Calif., Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: A prominent Technology Solutions Distributor, Sandler Partners, has introduced SCOUT email, an innovative feature available exclusively on SCOUT platform. This enhancement aims to assist Partners in delivering quicker responses to customers seeking Connectivity solutions. The launch took place in REDONDO BEACH, Calif., on Feb 17, 2026. Sandler Partners continues to prioritize efficient communication and service for its clientele.

data.title: NYC trial lawyer Eric Subin launches Subin LLP, continuing family legacy of 70+ years as top personal injury lawyers.
data.description: In New York on February 17, 2026, leading trial lawyer Eric Subin revealed the establishment of the redesigned law firm Subin LLP. This new firm upholds the longstanding Subin family reputation for excellence in representing clients as premier personal injury attorneys in New York for over seven decades. Specializing in various legal cases, Subin LLP is committed to offering top-notch legal services.
